topics.label: Application Design and Build
topics.description: Designing and building containerized applications for Kubernetes. Covers writing Dockerfiles, building container images, understanding multi-container pod patterns (sidecar, init, ambassador, adapter), and managing batch workloads with Jobs and CronJobs.

examScenarios.title: Build and run a multi-container pod with an init container
examScenarios.prompt: Create a pod that uses an init container to write a configuration file to a shared emptyDir volume, then starts a main nginx container that serves that file.
examScenarios.focus: initContainers spec, shared volumes, container ordering guarantees

examScenarios.title: Create a CronJob that runs a batch task on a schedule
examScenarios.prompt: Write a CronJob manifest that runs a Perl one-liner every 5 minutes, retains 3 successful and 1 failed job history, and sets a deadline of 100 seconds for each run.

topics.label: Application Deployment
topics.description: Deploying and updating applications using Deployments with rolling update strategies. Managing Helm releases, performing blue/green and canary deployments, and understanding rollback procedures.

examScenarios.title: Perform a rolling update and rollback
examScenarios.prompt: Update a Deployment's container image using kubectl set image, monitor the rollout status, then roll back to the previous revision after detecting errors in the new version.
examScenarios.focus: kubectl rollout status, kubectl rollout undo, maxSurge, maxUnavailable

examScenarios.title: Implement a canary deployment pattern
examScenarios.prompt: Given a stable Deployment with 5 replicas, create a canary Deployment with 1 replica using the same pod labels plus an additional 'track: canary' label, and configure the Service selector to send traffic to both.
examScenarios.focus: label selectors, multiple deployments behind a single Service, traffic splitting by replica count

topics.label: Application Observability and Maintenance
topics.description: Keeping applications healthy and diagnosable. Covers liveness, readiness, and startup probes, container logging patterns, monitoring basics, API deprecation handling, and debugging running pods.

studyPointers.label: Configure Liveness, Readiness and Startup Probes

examScenarios.title: Add probes to prevent traffic to an unready pod
examScenarios.prompt: Add a readiness probe that checks HTTP GET /healthz on port 8080 with an initial delay of 5s and a period of 10s. Add a liveness probe that execs 'cat /tmp/healthy' every 15s.
examScenarios.focus: readinessProbe vs livenessProbe, httpGet vs exec probe types, initialDelaySeconds, periodSeconds

examScenarios.title: Debug a crashing container without restarting it
examScenarios.prompt: A container in a pod is crash-looping. Use kubectl to retrieve its previous logs, inspect events, and attach an ephemeral debug container to investigate the filesystem.
examScenarios.focus: kubectl logs --previous, kubectl debug, kubectl describe pod events

topics.label: Application Environment, Configuration and Security
topics.description: Configuring applications securely using ConfigMaps and Secrets. Setting resource requests and limits, applying SecurityContext rules, working with ServiceAccounts, and understanding RBAC as it relates to application workloads.

studyPointers.label: Configure a Security Context for a Pod or Container
Resource Management for Pods and Containers

examScenarios.title: Inject configuration and secrets into a pod
examScenarios.prompt: Create a ConfigMap with an app.properties key and a Secret with a db-password key. Mount the ConfigMap as a volume at /etc/config and expose the Secret as an environment variable DB_PASSWORD.
examScenarios.focus: configMapKeyRef, secretKeyRef, envFrom, volumeMounts with configMap and secret volumes

examScenarios.title: Lock down a pod with SecurityContext
examScenarios.prompt: Configure a pod to run as user 1000, drop all Linux capabilities, prevent privilege escalation, and use a read-only root filesystem. Verify with kubectl exec.

topics.label: Services and Networking
topics.description: Exposing applications within and outside the cluster using Services of different types (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer). Routing external traffic with Ingress resources, controlling traffic flow with NetworkPolicies, and leveraging Kubernetes DNS for service discovery.

examScenarios.title: Expose a deployment and restrict ingress with a NetworkPolicy
examScenarios.prompt: Create a ClusterIP Service for a deployment on port 80. Then write a NetworkPolicy that allows only pods with label 'role=frontend' in the same namespace to reach the pods on port 8080. Verify with a test pod.
examScenarios.focus: Service selectors, podSelector, NetworkPolicy ingress rules, namespaceSelector

examScenarios.title: Route host-based traffic with an Ingress
examScenarios.prompt: Create an Ingress resource that routes api.example.com/v1 to service 'api-svc' on port 8080 and api.example.com/static to service 'static-svc' on port 80, using pathType Prefix.
examScenarios.focus: Ingress rules, host matching, pathType Prefix vs Exact, ingressClassName
